meaning: adj.卓越的,出众的,[宗](神)超然的,[哲]先验的
examples: There's no transcendent cosmic order or power.
It's a transcendent moment for me - a rebirth I always seem to need.
Sula said he was sorry we hadn't been able to find something more transcendent.
The deal was a transcendent moment for Karp, who created one of the world's busiest websites.
Recognize and reward those who adopt new behaviors and decision-making approaches based on transcendent priorities.
